task: Compute 3847 * 2913. Return only the final number, no words or units.
How many integers from 1 to 1000 inclusive are divisible by neither 3 nor 7? Return only the final number, no words or units.
constraints: exclude multiples of 3 and multiples of 7

task: Compute 6219 * 4807. Return only the final number, no words or units.
A principal of 10000 is invested at 5% annual interest compounded annually for 3 years. What is the final amount? Return only the final number, no words or units.
Compute 7 raised to the power 100, modulo 13 (that is, 7^100 mod 13). Return only the final number, no words or units.
How many days are there from 2021-03-01 to 2024-03-01 (counting the full span between these two dates)? Account for leap years. Return only the final number, no words or units.
constraints: span across 2024 which is a leap year
each year-to-year step is 365 or 366 days

task: Convert the hexadecimal number 0x3E8F to decimal (base 10). Return only the final number, no words or units.
Convert the binary number 110110101 to decimal (base 10). Return only the final number, no words or units.

A factory produces 1250 widgets per day. Each widget requires 3 screws and 2 bolts. Screws cost 0.04 each and bolts cost 0.07 each. Over 30 days, what is the total combined cost of screws and bolts? Return only the final number, no words or units.
constraints: chain per-day quantities, per-unit costs, and 30 days

task: In how many ways can a committee of 4 be chosen from 15 people if 2 specific people refuse to serve together? Return only the final number, no words or units.
constraints: total minus committees containing both specific people

task: A 24-hour digital clock currently shows 14:30. After exactly 1000 hours have passed, what will the hour reading be (an integer from 0 to 23)? Return only the final number, no words or units.
A product costs 80. Its price is increased by 25%, then decreased by 20%, then increased by 10%. What is the final price? Return only the final number, no words or units.
constraints: apply the three percentage changes in order

task: Compute 5832 * 3947. Return only the final number, no words or units.
How many integers from 1 to 500 inclusive are divisible by 4 or 6? Return only the final number, no words or units.
What is the sum of all multiples of 7 between 1 and 1000 inclusive? Return only the final number, no words or units.
Compute 12! / (8! * 4!). Return only the final number, no words or units.
Convert the base-7 number 6504 to decimal (base 10). Return only the final number, no words or units.
What is the least common multiple of 36 and 48? Return only the final number, no words or units.

A water tank holds 2400 liters. Pipe A fills it at 40 liters per minute while pipe B drains it at 15 liters per minute. Starting from empty with both pipes open, how many minutes does it take to fill the tank? Return only the final number, no words or units.
A bacteria population triples every 4 hours. If it starts at 50, what is the population after 24 hours? Return only the final number, no words or units.

An algorithm iterates once over an array of n elements, and for each element performs a binary search over that same array. What is its overall time complexity? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = O(n), b = O(n log n), c = O(n^2), d = O(log n). Output only the single word.
constraints: n iterations each doing a log n search

task: Is the number 221 prime or composite? Answer with exactly one of: prime, composite. Output only the single word.

A client requests a resource that requires authentication but provides no credentials at all. Which HTTP status code is the semantically correct response? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = 400, b = 401, c = 403, d = 404. Output only the single word.

In a POSIX shell, a process is terminated by the SIGKILL signal (signal number 9). What exit status does the shell report for that process? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = 9, b = 137, c = 128, d = 1. Output only the single word.

Consider the argument: 'Everyone I know is voting for this candidate, so they must be the best choice.' Which logical fallacy is this? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = ad hominem, b = bandwagon, c = straw man, d = false dilemma. Output only the single word.

A function computes the nth Fibonacci number using naive recursion fib(n)=fib(n-1)+fib(n-2) with no memoization. What is its time complexity? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = O(n), b = O(n^2), c = O(2^n), d = O(log n). Output only the single word.
Is the number 1763 prime or composite? Answer with exactly one of: prime, composite. Output only the single word.

Which loop correctly sums all integers from 1 to n inclusive into sum (initialized to 0)? Option a: for(i=1;i<n;i++) sum+=i;  Option b: for(i=1;i<=n;i++) sum+=i;  Option c: for(i=0;i<n;i++) sum+=i;  Option d: for(i=1;i<n;i--) sum+=i;  Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Output only the single word.
constraints: must include n and stop correctly

task: Which value is the largest: a = 0x1F (hexadecimal), b = 0o37 (octal), c = 0b11111 (binary), d = 31 (decimal)? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d, e. Where e = all four are equal. Output only the single word.
constraints: convert each to decimal and compare

task: In IEEE 754 double-precision floating point arithmetic, does the expression 0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3 evaluate to true? Answer with exactly one of: true, false. Output only the single word.
What is the worst-case time complexity of the quicksort algorithm? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = O(n log n), b = O(n^2), c = O(n), d = O(log n). Output only the single word.
Is the number 667 prime or composite? Answer with exactly one of: prime, composite. Output only the single word.

Consider the argument: 'If we allow students to redo one test, soon they will demand to redo every assignment and grades will become meaningless.' Which logical fallacy is this? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = slippery slope, b = ad hominem, c = circular reasoning, d = appeal to authority. Output only the single word.
Evaluate the bitwise expression (5 & 3) | (8 >> 1) and report its decimal value. Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = 4, b = 5, c = 1, d = 12. Output only the single word.
Which of these HTTP methods is NOT idempotent according to the HTTP specification? Answer with exactly one of: a, b, c, d. Where a = GET, b = PUT, c = DELETE, d = POST. Output only the single word.

Given the list of numbers [12, 7, 23, 4, 18, 9, 31, 6], return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"count": <how many values are strictly greater than 10>, "sum": <sum of the values strictly greater than 10>}.

Given these people with ages: Alice 30, Bob 25, Carol 35, Dave 28. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"oldest": "<lowercase name of the oldest>", "youngest": "<lowercase name of the youngest>", "averageAge": <average of the four ages>}.

Compute the factorials of 5 and 6 and their ratio. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"results": {"five": <5 factorial>, "six": <6 factorial>}, "ratio": <6 factorial divided by 5 factorial>}.

Consider the string 'deterministic evaluation harness'. Counting only alphabetic characters (ignore spaces), return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"vowels": <count of a,e,i,o,u>, "consonants": <count of alphabetic non-vowel characters>}.

Sales data: product A sold 3 units at 10 each, product B sold 5 units at 4 each, product C sold 2 units at 25 each. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"totalUnits": <sum of all units sold>, "totalRevenue": <sum of revenue across all products>, "topProduct": "<lowercase letter of the product with highest revenue>"}.
constraints: topProduct is a, b, or c lowercase

task: Consider the integers from 1 to 20 inclusive. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"primes": [<all prime numbers from 1 to 20 in ascending order>], "count": <how many primes>}.
Take the word 'algorithm'. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"reversed": "<the word reversed, lowercase>", "length": <number of characters in the word>}.

Consider the year 2024. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"year": 2024, "isLeap": "<the word true or false>", "daysInFebruary": <number of days in February that year>}.
constraints: isLeap is the lowercase word true or false

task: Transactions: id1 amount 50 credit, id2 amount 30 debit, id3 amount 70 credit, id4 amount 20 debit, id5 amount 40 credit. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"creditTotal": <sum of credit amounts>, "debitTotal": <sum of debit amounts>, "net": <creditTotal minus debitTotal>}.

Classify the numbers [4, 7, 12, 15, 22, 33, 8, 9] into even and odd, preserving original order. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"even": {"values": [<even numbers in original order>], "count": <count>}, "odd": {"values": [<odd numbers in original order>], "count": <count>}}.

In the string 'mississippi', count the occurrences of each distinct letter. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"m": <count>, "i": <count>, "s": <count>, "p": <count>}.

A restaurant bill is 80. Add 8.5% tax and an 18% tip, where the tip is computed on the pre-tax amount. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"tax": <tax amount>, "tip": <tip amount>, "total": <bill plus tax plus tip>}.

Consider the numbers 84 and 126. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"gcd": <greatest common divisor of 84 and 126>, "lcm": <least common multiple of 84 and 126>}.

Sort the words ['banana','apple','cherry','date'] in ascending alphabetical order. Return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"sorted": [<the words in ascending alphabetical order>], "first": "<first word after sorting>", "last": "<last word after sorting>"}.

Given the 3x3 grid [[1,2,3],[4,5,6],[7,8,9]], return only strict JSON (no markdown, no prose) with exactly this shape: {"rowSums": [<sum of each row, top to bottom>], "diagonalSum": <sum of the main diagonal (top-left to bottom-right)>, "total": <sum of all nine elements>}.
constraints: total is sum of all elements
